svg.text: FINPAY API — PHASE 7 ENGINEERING CHALLENGES
Design decisions and problems solved in the service layer
Prisma Decimal objects break JSON serialisation
design decision: Number() conversion at service boundary
Prisma returns Decimal objects for Decimal(18,2) fields. JSON.stringify() converts them to strings, not numbers,
Fix: every service method converts balance/amount via Number() before returning. Internal arithmetic uses new Decimal() for precision. Boundary is the service return value.
Race condition: two concurrent transfers can both pass the balance check
design decision: post-decrement negative balance guard
Two requests for R4,000 on a R5,000 balance could both read balance = 5000, both pass the check, and both execute — resulting in balance -R3,000.
Fix: after the debit inside the transaction block, check if updatedBalance < 0. If so, throw — which rolls back the debit automatically. Belt-and-suspenders on top of the pre-check.
Account enumeration attack via login error messages
design decision: unified error message
Returning "email not found" vs "wrong password" tells an attacker which emails are registered. They enumerate valid accounts before brute-forcing.
Fix: both "user not found" and "wrong password" return the same error: "Invalid email or password". Attacker learns nothing about whether the email exists.
Users must not be able to fetch other users' transaction details
design decision: ownership check before return
Transaction IDs are UUIDs — not sequential. Guessing is hard, but not impossible. A logged-in user could try known IDs.
Fix: getTransaction() verifies senderId === account.id OR receiverId === account.id. Returns 403 if neither matches. Record found but not yours.
Two sequential DB queries to get paginated list + total count
optimisation: Promise.all parallel fetch
getTransactionHistory needs both the page of records and the total count for pagination. Two awaits in sequence = two round trips to the database.
Fix: Promise.all([findMany(...), count(...)]) fires both queries simultaneously. One round trip instead of two. Measurable on large transaction histories.
User creation and wallet account creation must always succeed together or not at all
design decision: register() uses $transaction
If user.create succeeds but account.create fails, you have a user with no wallet. They can log in but have no account. A hard-to-debug partial state.
Fix: both creates are inside a single $transaction. If account.create fails, user.create rolls back. No orphaned users. No walletless accounts.
red = bug prevented
yellow = security concern
blue = performance / correctness decision
